description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, bulging body and flat, circular ends. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held tightly together by several horizontal metal hoops that are weathered and slightly rusted. The wood appears aged, with a grayish-brown patina and visible grain, suggesting long-term exposure to the elements. One side of the barrel features a small, round wooden bung, while another side displays a faint rectangular marking or stamp, possibly indicating a date or origin. The top and bottom surfaces are flush with the staves and reinforced by the metal bands. The overall appearance is robust and utilitarian, suitable for storage or transport of liquids or dry goods in an urban or industrial setting.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops, a bung on one side, and faint markings, showing signs of age and outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden bung protruding from the side and the texture of the wood and metal hoops.
description_view_1: Side view with a visible rectangular marking or stamp and the bung partially visible on the left.
description_view_2: Rear view with uninterrupted wooden staves and metal hoops, no bung or markings visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the bung visible on the right, no markings present.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: rectangular stamp with faint text and symbols
